metadata.title: Rijksmuseum Suriname Collection
metadata.description: Explore the Suriname collection of the Rijksmuseum — paintings, prints, photographs, maps and objects connected to the colonial history of Suriname.

home.title: Suriname Collection
home.subtitle: Rijksmuseum Amsterdam
home.intro: Explore {count} objects from the Rijksmuseum connected to Suriname — from 17th-century plantation paintings to 20th-century documentary photography.

events.1667: Treaty of Breda — Suriname becomes Dutch
events.1954: Suriname becomes autonomous within the Kingdom

geoposition.title: Place on Map
geoposition.subtitle: Help enrich the collection by placing objects on the map with a camera position and viewing angle
geoposition.triageTitle: Does this image show a recognisable outdoor location?
geoposition.triageDescription: We're looking for images that depict a real outdoor scene with buildings, streets, landscapes, or waterways. Indoor scenes, abstract art, portraits, and non-realistic drawings should be skipped.
geoposition.triageYes: Yes — it shows an outdoor location
geoposition.problemPlaceholder: Describe the issue (e.g. image doesn't load, wrong metadata…)
geoposition.locationDescription: Sort the geographic keywords: move keywords that are visible in the image to the green column, and keywords not visible to the grey column.
geoposition.dragHere: Click keywords below to move them here
geoposition.moveHere: Click keywords above to move them here
geoposition.cameraDescription: Drag the camera marker to where the artist or photographer would have stood. Adjust the viewing cone to match the field of view in the image.
geoposition.cameraInstructions: Drag the red marker to reposition. Drag the white handle to rotate. Drag the yellow handles to adjust the field of view. Right-click to teleport the camera.
geoposition.successDescription: Thank you for your contribution. Your geo-position has been submitted for review.
geoposition.allDoneDescription: Amazing work — every object in the queue has been placed on the map. Check back later for new objects.
